prevalence.trend: stable; high social stigma in some regions

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis of depigmented macules; Wood lamp to confirm

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; response depends on early treatment and phototherapy access

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in dermatology; Wood lamp in specialty clinics
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Topical steroids or calcineurin inhibitors, phototherapy (NB-UVB), camouflage; systemic therapy for extensive disease
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Stigma and limited phototherapy access outside metros
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Large dermatology workforce in urban centers and availability of topical treatments
